displayName: USA – Spouse / Fiancé Visa (K-1 / CR-1 / IR-1)
shortDescription: USCIS family immigration · K-1 fiancé or CR-1/IR-1 spouse · NVC & interview

policyAlert.summary: Confirm current USCIS I-130/I-129F fees and NVC fees on uscis.gov and travel.state.gov before quoting.

alert.title: Pathway selection matters
alert.body: K-1 (fiancé, marry in US) vs CR-1/IR-1 (already married, immigrant visa) have different timelines and steps. Counsel correctly before petition filing.

about.label: Description
about.value: Family-based immigration for foreign spouse or fiancé of US citizen. CR-1/IR-1 for married couples (immigrant visa); K-1 for fiancés to marry in US within 90 days of entry.

about.label: Eligible applicants
about.value: US citizen petitioner · Valid marriage or bona fide engagement · Genuine relationship · Medical and police clearances · Affidavit of support

about.label: K-1 vs CR-1/IR-1
about.value: K-1: not yet married, faster initial petition but must marry in US and adjust status. CR-1/IR-1: already married, immigrant visa with conditional PR if marriage under 2 years.

about.label: Key authority
about.value: USCIS · National Visa Center (NVC) · US Embassy New Delhi/Mumbai/Chennai/Hyderabad/Kolkata

about.label: After approval
about.value: Enter US as immigrant (CR-1/IR-1) or K-1 then marry and file I-485. Understand conditional green card rules if applicable.

eligibility.criterion: US citizen petitioner (not green card holder for K-1)
eligibility.note: LPR sponsors spouse via different family preference—confirm category

eligibility.criterion: Legally valid marriage or qualifying engagement
eligibility.note: Marriage cert or proof of meeting in person within 2 years for K-1
Petitioner income 125% poverty guideline or joint sponsor

redFlagsBanner: If denied at embassy for relationship concerns, do not refile without addressing specific concerns and strengthening evidence.

redFlags.title: Bona fide relationship not credible
redFlags.description: Minimal contact history, large age gap unexplained, or marriage soon after online-only relationship.
redFlags.fix: Document meeting history, visits, communication logs, joint financial plans

redFlags.title: Insufficient I-864 income
redFlags.description: Petitioner income below 125% poverty line without joint sponsor.
redFlags.fix: Joint sponsor with tax returns, or petitioner additional evidence of assets

redFlags.title: Wrong pathway chosen
redFlags.description: K-1 filed when already married, or CR-1 when not yet married.
redFlags.fix: Reassess marital status and timeline before petition

redFlags.title: Incomplete civil documents
redFlags.description: Missing divorce decrees, name change proof, or police certificates.
redFlags.fix: NVC checklist review; obtain certified translations

redFlags.title: Prior immigration violations
redFlags.description: Overstay, misrepresentation, or prior visa refusals not disclosed.
redFlags.fix: Full disclosure; consult on waivers if needed

redFlags.title: Public charge or medical inadmissibility
redFlags.description: Medical exam findings or insufficient support without explanation.
redFlags.fix: Complete medical early; secure joint sponsor if income weak

faqs.q: K-1 or CR-1—which is better?
faqs.a: Depends on whether couple is already married and timeline preferences. CR-1/IR-1 grants immigrant visa directly; K-1 requires marriage in US then adjustment. No universal 'better'—assess case.

faqs.q: Can we guarantee approval?
faqs.a: No. USCIS, NVC, and embassy each review. Genuine relationships can still face delays or requests for evidence.

faqs.q: How long total process?
faqs.a: Often 12–24 months depending on pathway, service center, and embassy backlog. Check current processing times on uscis.gov.

faqs.q: Can fiancé work on K-1?
faqs.a: K-1 must marry within 90 days and file for work authorisation through adjustment of status—cannot work immediately on entry.

faqs.q: What is conditional green card?
faqs.a: If marriage under 2 years at entry, CR-1 recipient gets 2-year conditional card; must file I-751 to remove conditions.

faqs.q: Is joint sponsor allowed?
faqs.a: Yes, when petitioner income insufficient for I-864. Joint sponsor must meet income requirements and submit tax returns.

faqs.q: What if petitioner was previously married?
faqs.a: Must provide divorce decrees or death certificates for all prior marriages—both petitioner and beneficiary.

faqs.q: Can beneficiary visit on B2 during processing?
faqs.a: Risky—214(b) applies and immigrant intent can cause B2 refusal. Counsel honestly on risks.

faqs.q: What is Description for this service?
faqs.a: Family-based immigration for foreign spouse or fiancé of US citizen. CR-1/IR-1 for married couples (immigrant visa); K-1 for fiancés to marry in US within 90 days of entry.

faqs.q: What is Eligible applicants for this service?
faqs.a: US citizen petitioner · Valid marriage or bona fide engagement · Genuine relationship · Medical and police clearances · Affidavit of support

faqs.q: What is K-1 vs CR-1/IR-1 for this service?
faqs.a: K-1: not yet married, faster initial petition but must marry in US and adjust status. CR-1/IR-1: already married, immigrant visa with conditional PR if marriage under 2 years.

faqs.q: Important note on K-1 vs CR-1/IR-1?
faqs.a: K-1: not yet married, faster initial petition but must marry in US and adjust status. CR-1/IR-1: already married, immigrant visa with conditional PR if marriage under 2 years.

faqs.q: What is Key authority for this service?
faqs.a: USCIS · National Visa Center (NVC) · US Embassy New Delhi/Mumbai/Chennai/Hyderabad/Kolkata

faqs.q: What is After approval for this service?
faqs.a: Enter US as immigrant (CR-1/IR-1) or K-1 then marry and file I-485. Understand conditional green card rules if applicable.

faqs.q: Is US citizen petitioner (not green card holder for K-1) required?
faqs.a: LPR sponsors spouse via different family preference—confirm category

faqs.q: Is Legally valid marriage or qualifying engagement required?
faqs.a: Marriage cert or proof of meeting in person within 2 years for K-1

faqs.q: Is Genuine relationship evidence required?
faqs.a: Photos, communication, visits, joint plans

faqs.q: Is I-864 affidavit of support required?
faqs.a: Petitioner income 125% poverty guideline or joint sponsor

faqs.q: Is Civil documents (birth, marriage, police) required?
faqs.a: NVC document collection phase

faqs.q: Is Medical exam by panel physician required?
faqs.a: Before embassy interview

faqs.q: Is DS-260 and embassy interview required?
faqs.a: Final stage before visa issuance

faqs.q: What is the Processing time?
faqs.a: 12–24m — USCIS + NVC + embassy

faqs.q: What is the Government fee?
faqs.a: USD $1,000+ — Petition + NVC + medical

faqs.q: What is the Our approval rate?
faqs.a: 88% — ~78% industry benchmark

faqs.q: What is the Required docs?
faqs.a: 20 — + civil documents

faqs.q: What is the Consultancy fee?
faqs.a: See fee tab — + govt & medical

faqs.q: What if the client has Bona fide relationship not credible?
faqs.a: Document meeting history, visits, communication logs, joint financial plans

faqs.q: Why is Bona fide relationship not credible a problem?
faqs.a: Minimal contact history, large age gap unexplained, or marriage soon after online-only relationship.

faqs.q: What if the client has Insufficient I-864 income?
faqs.a: Joint sponsor with tax returns, or petitioner additional evidence of assets

faqs.q: Why is Insufficient I-864 income a problem?
faqs.a: Petitioner income below 125% poverty line without joint sponsor.

compliance: Complex immigration petitions may require licensed US immigration attorney—refer when appropriate
Never guarantee green card or visa approval
Client must sign all petitions truthfully
Separate consultancy, USCIS, NVC, medical, and embassy fees in quotes
proTips: Choose K-1 vs CR-1/IR-1 at intake—changing later is costly
Build relationship evidence binder from day one of engagement
Track USCIS and NVC case numbers in shared client portal
Prepare beneficiary for embassy interview on relationship history
Verify I-864 income early; identify joint sponsor if needed
postApproval: Explain entry rules and 90-day marriage deadline for K-1
Remind CR-1/IR-1 entrants of conditional vs immediate 10-year card
Guide on SSN, I-485 or I-751 timeline as applicable
Connect to settlement resources if offered

staffNotes.author: Documentation team
staffNotes.text: Always confirm whether couple is legally married before choosing I-130 vs I-129F—errors require refiling.

staffNotes.author: Quality
staffNotes.text: I-864 income check mandatory at intake before petition fee collection.

quiz.question: K-1 visa is for:
options: Married couples only
Fiancés to marry in US within 90 days
Students
Workers
quiz.explanation: K-1 is fiancé visa; marriage must occur in US within 90 days.

quiz.question: CR-1/IR-1 is for:
options: Engaged couples only
Already married spouses of US citizens
Tourists
Employers
quiz.explanation: Immigrant visa for foreign spouse of US citizen.

quiz.question: I-864 affidavit requires petitioner income generally at:
options: 50% poverty line
125% of poverty guideline
No minimum
USD $1 million
quiz.explanation: Standard financial sponsorship threshold.

quiz.question: Can counselors guarantee spouse visa approval?
options: Yes
No, never
Only for K-1
Only if married 10 years
quiz.explanation: Multi-stage review; no guarantees permitted.

quiz.question: After K-1 entry, beneficiary must:
options: Leave immediately
Marry petitioner within 90 days
Start work without permit
Skip medical exam
quiz.explanation: K-1 condition is marriage within 90 days of US entry.

quiz.explanation: NVC document completeness

dos: Confirm marital status before choosing petition type
Build relationship evidence from engagement onward
Verify I-864 income or secure joint sponsor early
Track USCIS and NVC stages with client
Prepare beneficiary for embassy relationship questions
donts: Do not guarantee immigrant visa approval
Do not file K-1 if couple is already married
Do not hide prior marriages or immigration violations
Do not advise B2 visit during pending immigrant petition without risk disclosure
Do not submit uncertified translations for civil documents
mistakes: Insufficient relationship photos and visit evidence
Missing divorce decree from prior marriage
I-864 without joint sponsor when income insufficient
Wrong embassy document format at NVC stage
Beneficiary unprepared for interview relationship timeline

sampleDocs.title: Sample passport bio page (mock)
sampleDocs.description: Show clients which pages to scan — MRZ visible, no glare, full spread.

sampleDocs.title: Sample marriage certificate (mock)
sampleDocs.description: Registered marriage proof — explain translation and notarization if needed.

sampleDocs.title: Sample relationship timeline & photos guide (mock)
sampleDocs.description: Collage layout for chat logs, travel photos, and ceremony pictures.

sampleDocs.title: Sample sponsor / undertaking letter (mock)
sampleDocs.description: Sponsor income, accommodation, and maintenance commitment.

sampleDocs.title: Sample bank statement (mock)
sampleDocs.description: 6-month statement with name, account number, and closing balance — explain seasoning.

sampleDocs.title: Sample chat / communication evidence (mock)
sampleDocs.description: Redacted WhatsApp or email excerpts — privacy-safe specimen.

applicant.summary: CR-1/IR-1 immigrant visa grants permanent residence and unrestricted work authorization upon entry/adjustment.
details: K-1 fiancé visa requires EAD after marriage/AOS for work.

spouse.summary: US citizen petitioner has full work rights.
